message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Monterey Crossings (Chick-fil-A, Dickey's Barbecue Pit, Pokehana) (Palm Desert)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Cluster-level: Chick-fil-A ~210 Yelp reviews / ~638 Google reviews; Pokehana (Hwy 111 PD location, not Monterey Crossings pad) 229 Yelp reviews; Dickey's Palm Desert location reviews limited/dispersed [UNVERIFIED whether a Dickey's pad operates specifically at Monterey Crossings vs. nearby corridor]
- Sentiment: Mixed to mostly positive — Chick-fil-A 3.4-3.9 stars (mixed: service inconsistency, long waits flagged); Pokehana 4.4 stars (positive on freshness and service); Dickey's generally positive in regional listings
- Longevity: Development cluster: Chick-fil-A opened July 14, 2022; Monterey Crossings specific plan approved May 2017; cluster still building out with 130-room hotel pending [UNVERIFIED exact opening dates for Dickey's and Pokehana pads at Monterey Crossings specifically]
- Credentials: []
- Appears in queries: Individual chain tenants surface for branded queries ('Chick-fil-A Palm Desert', 'Pokehana Palm Desert', 'Dickey's Palm Springs area'). The cluster name 'Monterey Crossings' surfaces in commercial real estate queries (LoopNet, Hanley Investment, KDC Construction) and trade press (Palm Springs Life Vision, GPS Business Insider) — not in consumer dining queries. Does NOT surface for 'best restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Desert', 'date night Rancho Mirage'. May surface for 'fast food near I-10 Palm Desert' but as individual chain pins, not as a cluster.
- Site structured data: Monterey Crossings has no consumer-facing site — only developer/leasing pages (KDC Construction, Hanley Investment, LoopNet) and adwarchitects portfolio page. Tenant sites: Chick-fil-A and Dickey's run on robust corporate domains with location structured data. Pokehana's site (pokehana.com) lacks visible JSON-LD/schema.org markup; hours and locations are present but not machine-structured.
- Visibility gap score: 2
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about restaurants at Monterey Crossings in Palm Desert:

Monterey Crossings is an 18-acre, 130,000-square-foot project at the northeast corner of Dinah Shore Drive and Monterey Avenue that has recently seen its first businesses open including Chick-fil-A and other fast casual dining options.

The specific restaurants that have been identified at Monterey Crossings include:

- Chick-Fil-A and The Habit
- Jersey Mike's Subs, Nekter Juice Bar, and Roll-Em-Up Taquitos
- Dickey's Barbecue Pit and Pokehana

A 130-room, four-story hotel is still in the works for the site.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Palm Desert:

## Top Fine Dining Restaurants in Palm Desert

**Mastro's Steakhouse**
Mastro's Steakhouse Palm Desert provides a sophisticated dining experience and is an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025, continuing to be a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley. It's synonymous with luxury dining and offers an exceptional experience for steak lovers with an elegant atmosphere complete with live music and dim lighting, creating a sophisticated dining environment.

**Jillian's**
Jillian's is a sophisticated and romantic restaurant offering an upscale dining experience with European cuisine, featuring a charming courtyard and live music, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere. The menu showcases elevated Italian dishes including house-made pastas, prime meats, and seafood, complemented by an extensive wine selection.

**Pacifica Seafood Restaurant**
Pacifica's second-story location provides stunning views of the desert landscape, making it ideal for a romantic dinner, with excellent food, attentive service, and a welcoming atmosphere.

**Morgan's in the Desert**
Morgan's in the desert showcases contemporary American cuisine inspired by the bounty of the Coachella Valley, sourcing the region's top products and crafting them with traditional cooking methods.

**Ruth's Chris Steak House**
Ruth's Chris serves the finest USDA Prime beef and offers New Orleans-inspired appetizers, fresh seafood, and homemade desserts in a warm, inviting atmosphere with genuine hospitality.

Other notable options include Il Corso, Ristorante Mamma Gina (Italian), and Castelli's Ristorante.

A new Chick-fil-A restaurant began serving Palm Desert and the Coachella Valley on Thursday, July 14, 2022. Located at 73070 Dinah Shore Drive, Palm Desert, CA, the restaurant is open for dine-in, drive-thru and mobile drive-thru from 6:30 a.m. to 10 p.m., Monday through Saturday.

Based on the search results, here's what I found:

**Dickey's Barbecue Pit Location:**
Dickey's Barbecue Pit is located at 72600 Dinah Shore Drive, Palm Desert, California 92211. Since 1941, Dickey's Barbecue Pit has been perfecting the art of barbecue, and has been catering Texas barbecue for over 60 years.

**About Dinah Shore Drive:**
Dinah Shore Drive in Palm Desert is named after the iconic entertainer Dinah Shore. The Dinah Shore Palm Springs Estate was designed in 1964 by famed "desert modern" architect Donald Wexler for actress, singer and television personality Dinah Shore. Dinah Shore was an American singer, actor, TV personality, and chart-topping female vocalist of the 1940s.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent date night restaurants in the Palm Desert and Coachella Valley area:

## Popular Date Night Destinations

Grapefruit Basil in Indian Wells is an ideal spot for romance, while this stylish restaurant sets the mood for romance in paradise with alfresco dining, island-inspired cocktails, and dishes made with ingredients grown on the property gardens.

Morgan's in the Desert is a perfect spot for a date night with its elegant and romantic ambiance, it's the perfect setting for a special evening.

## Steakhouse Options

Sullivan's Steakhouse in Palm Desert features hand-cut USDA Prime steaks and fresh seafood with live jazz floating through the dining room.

## Diverse Cuisines

Mikado at the JW Marriott Desert Springs Resort & Spa is known throughout the Coachella Valley and Southern California as the leader in authentic Teppan-Yaki Dining.

The Bistro specializes in exciting, contemporary fine cuisine including succulent seafood and tantalizing sushi selections, as well as carefully dry-aged on site, hand-carved, premium USDA beef.

## Entertainment & Dining Combinations

Fantasy Springs offers multiple options for date night from its year-round entertainment lineup and lively casino floor, to multiple onsite dining options, a championship golf course, and even a 24-lane bowling alley.

These restaurants offer a range of atmospheres and cuisines perfect for a romantic evening in the Coachella Valley area.

content.text: Dickey's Palm Desert at 72600 Dinah Shore Drive does exist — likely on or near the Monterey Crossings pad. Now I have enough to evaluate the claims.

confirms: Chick-fil-A Palm Desert opened July 14, 2022 at 73070 Dinah Shore Dr (Monterey Crossings pad) — confirmed via Chick-fil-A press release and KESQ coverage.
Chick-fil-A Palm Desert has approximately 210 Yelp reviews — confirmed via Yelp listing title.

Pokehana Palm Desert (229 Yelp reviews) is at 73405 Hwy 111 Ste 104 — NOT the Monterey Crossings pad. The claim correctly flagged this distinction. Pokehana is reported as a future/expected tenant at Monterey Crossings per Palm Springs Life Vision coverage.
Pokehana.com lacks JSON-LD/schema.org structured data — confirmed via direct HTML inspection. Hours and locations present as plain HTML.

Monterey Crossings cluster does NOT surface in consumer dining queries — 'best fine dining Palm Desert' and 'date night Coachella Valley' results return Mastro's, Jillian's, Pacifica, Morgan's in the Desert, Ruth's Chris, Sullivan's, Grapefruit Basil, etc. Zero mention of Monterey Crossings tenants.
Monterey Crossings surfaces in commercial real estate (LoopNet, CommercialCafe, Hanley/Shopping Center Business) and trade press (Palm Springs Life Vision, Patch) — confirmed.
18-acre development, 130-room hotel pending, ~130,000 sq ft commercial — confirmed via multiple sources.
Monterey Crossings has no consumer-facing site — only developer/leasing pages — confirmed.
contradicts: The claim flags 'whether a Dickey's pad operates specifically at Monterey Crossings vs. nearby corridor' as UNVERIFIED — actually verifiable: a Dickey's Barbecue Pit operates at 72600 Dinah Shore Drive, Palm Desert (the Monterey Crossings corridor, adjacent to Chick-fil-A at 73070 Dinah Shore). So Dickey's pad presence at/near Monterey Crossings is confirmed, not unverified.

Tenant list is incomplete: Patch and Palm Springs Life report The Habit, Jersey Mike's Subs, Nekter Juice Bar, and Roll-Em-Up Taquitos as Monterey Crossings tenants — the brief frames the cluster around only Chick-fil-A/Dickey's/Pokehana, understating cluster scope.
flagged_as_unverified: Chick-fil-A 3.4-3.9 star rating range — one source cited 3.9, another 3.3; could not pin exact current Yelp star rating.
Chick-fil-A ~638 Google reviews — could not independently confirm the Google review count via search.
Pokehana 4.4 star rating — not independently observed in search snippets.
Dickey's Palm Desert review counts and star ratings — could not retrieve specific numbers.

Whether Chick-fil-A and Dickey's corporate sites carry JSON-LD location structured data — WebFetch's HTML rendering said no on both, but corporate chains typically do; the tool's markdown conversion strips script tags, so this is inconclusive rather than refuted.
Exact opening dates for Dickey's and Pokehana at Monterey Crossings pads — Pokehana's primary location remains Hwy 111; Dickey's at 72600 Dinah Shore exists but opening date not confirmed.
Monterey Crossings specific plan approval date of May 2017 — not independently verified in this pass.
input.notes: Core thesis is sound: Monterey Crossings is an anchor-reference development that surfaces in CRE/trade press but not in consumer dining queries, and the cluster has no consolidated consumer web presence (only individual chain pins). The visibility gap score of 2 and anchor-reference tier are well-supported. Two issues: (1) the brief unnecessarily flagged Dickey's pad existence as UNVERIFIED when it is verifiable at 72600 Dinah Shore Drive (same corridor as Chick-fil-A's 73070), and (2) the tenant inventory is incomplete — The Habit, Jersey Mike's, Nekter, and Roll-Em-Up Taquitos are also documented Monterey Crossings tenants. Specific review counts and star ratings should be marked unverified until directly observed on Yelp/Google listing pages.
